niche: WorthScan used desk chair resale valuation

source_inputs.label: Operator brief
source_inputs.value: Create a WorthScan post for a used ergonomic desk chair listing.

source_inputs.label: Valuation method
source_inputs.value: Check brand/model, adjustment controls, mesh/foam wear, cylinder height, wheel condition, pickup hassle, and three local furniture comps.

source_inputs.label: Research boundary
source_inputs.value: Use only reviewed manual examples. Do not scrape furniture listings or social platforms.

trend_examples.hook: This desk chair price depends on one label
trend_examples.notes: Brand/model reveal works well for furniture valuation.

trend_examples.hook: Pickup hassle is part of the price
trend_examples.notes: Hassle cost gives furniture posts a practical angle.
Condition crops support the final range.

notes: No live provider calls or social automation.
Human approval required before manual posting.

slides.on_screen_text: Scan this desk chair before pickup
slides.visual_direction: Show the chair, asking price, and pickup context.

slides.on_screen_text: Find brand, controls, cylinder, and wear
slides.visual_direction: Crop the label, armrests, wheels, mesh, and adjustment controls.
Show comp cards with brand, condition, and pickup distance.

slides.on_screen_text: Subtract stains, broken levers, and pickup hassle
Worth range: steal, fair, or pass

output_requirements.caption: Desk chair deal or pickup regret? Comment "scan" with the next listing. WorthScan estimates range, not guaranteed value.
output_requirements.spoken_script: A used desk chair is all about model, condition, and pickup hassle. Confirm the brand and controls, check wear and cylinder risk, compare three local listings, then call the price a steal, fair, or pass.
posting_notes: Do not claim ergonomic or safety condition beyond visible evidence.
Keep the range and disclaimer visible.

qa_notes: Furniture pickup risk is a value factor, not a health claim.
